secondary_archetype: Data Platform / ML Data Infrastructure TPM

level_signals: 8+ years in data TPM explicitly required
Responsibility to build, coach, and grow a TPM team
Comp range $259,200–$312,400 base consistent with Lead/Staff IC
'Lead the function' language implies people leadership scope

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years in data: Deep, hands-on experience delivering data programs as a technical program manager - data platform, pipelines, enrichment / curation, labelling - including people and process leadership. You've built or scaled a program function and you get things done with ownership and a bias for action.
People leadership: You build, coach, and grow high-performing teams, and raise the bar on the craft.

Technically strong: Credible with engineers on data platforms, pipelines, and large-scale data infrastructure, even though you won't write production code. Experience with distributed data platforms at Petabyte scale.
Experience with data ingestion from raw data to processing through different stages and pipelines. Understands Directed Acyclic Graphs, their usage and challenges.
Solid understanding of Machine Learning, GPUs and training and inference of 500M-20B parameter models.
Proficient with some or all of Databricks, Flyte, Ray, Kubernetes, Azure, Docker, Python.
Proficient with AI Agents to accelerate execution: eg. Cursor, Claude, Codex.
Stays neutral and adapts under pressure: Effective in ambiguous, fast-moving environments; you flex your style and bring structure without slowing delivery.
Systems thinking: You reason about how the parts of a complex data system fit together, and how a change in one area ripples into others.
Product-minded: You focus on outcomes and the people your programs serve - prioritising by impact and defining what good looks like, not just tracking activity.
Exceptional cross-functional leadership: You align and influence across data, ML, and engineering without relying on authority.
Strategic & business impact: You link data investment to measurable outcomes - quality, throughput, cost.
Clear, data-driven communicator: You set direction with clarity and steer with the right metrics.
Growth mindset: Open to feedback and always looking to improve.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Working experience with a large scale distributed data platform used by 100s of engineers and researchers.
Background in autonomous vehicles, robotics, or another large-scale data / ML program.
An engineering or computer science degree, or experience working as an engineer; fluent with modern delivery tooling (e.g. Jira).

scope.team_size_estimate: small TPM team (exact size not stated)
scope.surface: Data org — data platform, ingestion, standardisation pipelines, enrichment, curation, and labelling for the AI Driver; also supports simulation and validation teams
scope.business_model: B2B — AI software and foundation models for automakers (automated driving systems)
core_responsibilities: Build, coach, and grow a small TPM team; hire to fill gaps and raise the bar
Partner with Data leadership on planning, prioritisation, and execution across the data roadmap
Maintain partnership with Engineering leaders to land big outcomes
Drive flagship programs directly: data platform, data pipelines, enrichment & curation, labelling/annotation, dataset management and quality
Establish scalable practices: planning cadences, governance, KPIs and dashboards, escalations and operational reviews
Align cross-functionally with data, ML, engineering, and external annotation suppliers; manage dependencies, risk, and trade-offs
Tie delivery to data quality, throughput, cost, and time-to-insight; represent the data org in company-level reviews

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly stated ($259,200–$312,400 base) plus equity
Clear ownership scope and measurable success criteria (data quality, throughput, cost)
Role has people management scope with explicit team-building mandate
JD explicitly encourages candidates who don't meet every requirement to apply
Company at frontier of AV/Embodied AI — high-impact technical domain
red_flags: No visa sponsorship information stated — unclear for international candidates
Hybrid in Sunnyvale, CA only — eliminates fully remote candidates
Broad technical requirements (Petabyte-scale infra, DAGs, 500M-20B parameter models, multiple cloud/orchestration tools) may be difficult to fully satisfy
Office time requirement for hybrid not specified — ambiguity around in-office expectations

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, 8+ years requirement, and people management scope)
scope.team_size_estimate (JD says 'small TPM team' but no headcount given)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities and delivery framing; not a discrete list in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description — B2B automaker-facing AI software)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred as false from 'full-time role based in Sunnyvale, CA (hybrid)' language)
location.visa_sponsorship (not mentioned in JD body; marked unclear)
ambiguities: The JD lists 14 bullets under 'Essential' but explicitly states 'The first four are our must-haves.' Items 5–14 under Essential are therefore ambiguous — they appear as unconditional bullets but the JD implies only the first four are strict gates. All Essential items are classified as hard severity here since they appear in the required section without 'preferred' language; however the first four carry special emphasis per the JD.
Office time percentage for hybrid role is not specified — only 'hybrid' is stated.
Visa sponsorship policy not mentioned; left as 'unclear'.
